title: Sailing Past the Edge and Discovering the Island
speaker: Tarek Anous
abstract: Quantum extremal surfaces and islands have led us to rethink the very meaning of microscopic entropy in the presence of dynamical gravity. I will present some work elucidating how the island/QES prescription emerges in a simple doubly holographic setup where we have analytic control. We will consider two holographic 2d CFTs joined at an interface  such that the bulk dual is a geometry that interpolates between two locally 3d AdS geometries with different AdS lengths. In particular we will consider systems where the transition region is a thin brane with tension T.

proof_stages.label: Hybrid brane formulation and entropy target
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames the system as two CFTs joined through an interface/brane theory obtained by integrating out the bulk, and identifies the desired observable as the RT entropy of a quantum-dot-plus-bath region to be reinterpreted from the brane perspective.

proof_stages.label: Bottom-up thin-interface gravitational model
proof_stages.summary: To make the brane picture precise, the speaker chooses a bottom-up action with two Euclidean AdS Einstein-Hilbert pieces, an interface extrinsic-curvature term, and a tension term modeling the degrees of freedom at the quantum dot.

proof_stages.label: Exact AdS2-sliced solution and junction regularity
proof_stages.summary: The equations of motion admit simple AdS2-sliced geometries in which the brane sits at a tension-determined angle, the allowed tension lies in a finite range, and the Israel junction conditions imply that RT geodesics cross the interface smoothly, in the C1 sense.

proof_stages.label: Full RT geodesic entropy formula
proof_stages.summary: Using the smooth cross-brane geodesic, the entropy of an asymmetric interval containing the dot and bath segments is computed as a complicated circular-arc length formula, then checked to depend on the expected boundary conformal cross-ratio and inversion symmetry.

proof_stages.label: Near-critical brane limit and generalized-entropy match
proof_stages.summary: In the limit where the tension approaches its maximum and the interface approaches the asymptotic boundary, the brane theory behaves like a cutoff CFT coupled to a simple quantum-gravity degree of freedom, and extremizing the sum of CFT entropies over a common brane point reproduces the finite part of the full RT answer as the cutoff parameter goes to zero.

proof_stages.label: Conformal maps to TFD and island/Page transition
proof_stages.summary: A sequence of conformal transformations maps the flat interface setup to a circle and then to a thermal-field-double/eternal-black-hole geometry, where the same formulas show an island region and a Page-curve-like transition between competing geodesic or OPE channels despite the absence of an area term.

proof_stages.label: Comparison, extensions, and CFT-side derivations
proof_stages.summary: The closing discussion compares the construction with existing island models, notes that explicit JT/Einstein terms or higher-dimensional analogues could be added, and explores whether the messy cross-ratio dependence could be derived directly by replica/interface-CFT or conformal-welding methods.
